title: Measure remote consumers and operator view usage
goal: Close the audit evidence gap by inventorying remote runtimes, actual source consumers and usage frequency for Cabinet, Leitstand, Schauwerk, Chronik, Heimlern and Vibe-Lab before shutdown or consolidation decisions.

acceptance.assertion: Local and registered remote hosts are checked for relevant services, timers and deployment endpoints with source and timestamp.
Each claimed consumer is classified as runtime-observed, source-integrated, declared-only or absent.
Operator views expose a bounded, privacy-preserving usage or access signal sufficient to distinguish active from unused surfaces.
Shutdown and consolidation tasks consume this evidence and preserve uncertainty where measurement is unavailable.

metadata.outcome: All four registered locations received bounded coverage, nine claimed consumer surfaces were classified, and a privacy-preserving access/activity snapshot was merged into Cabinet without changing any service or runtime.

validated_acceptance.remote-runtime: All four registered locations are represented with an observation timestamp and an explicit observed, partial or unknown coverage class; one unreachable and one attribution-limited location remain uncertainty rather than false negatives.
validated_acceptance.consumer-classification: All nine claimed surfaces are uniquely classified as runtime-observed, source-integrated, declared-only or absent and are bound to a twelve-repository source snapshot.
validated_acceptance.usage-measure: Bounded service-journal and activity aggregates distinguish recent access, automation-only activity, runtime without access evidence and no observed runtime activity without exposing routes, clients or content.
validated_acceptance.decision-input: The merged snapshot permits repository-only T004 consolidation, while T013 still requires explicit shutdown authorization, rollback and fresh preflight and T014 remains dependency-gated.

measurement_limits: Service presence is not human usage.
Automated activity is not human usage.
Browser-history aggregation was not admitted after the bounded probe failed unreliably.
One registered location was unreachable and one was only partially attributable.
Source references prove integration paths, not successful semantic consumption.
